records.ety: [F. procurer, L. procurare, procuratum, to take care of; pro for + curare to take care, fr. cura care. See Cure, and cf. Proctor, Proxy.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Procured (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Procuring.]

defs: To bring into possession; to cause to accrue to, or to come into possession of; to acquire or provide for one's self or for another; to gain; to get; to obtain by any means, as by purchase or loan.
quotes: If we procure not to ourselves more woe.

defs: To contrive; to bring about; to effect; to cause.
quotes: By all means possible they procure to have gold and silver among them in reproach.
Proceed, Solinus, to procure my fall.
The famous Briton prince and faery knight, . . . Of the fair Alma greatly were procured To make there longer sojourn and abode.

defs: To cause to come; to bring; to attract.
quotes: What unaccustomed cause procures her hither?

defs: To obtain for illicit intercourse or prostitution.
To manage business for another in court.
